# Copilot / AI-agent operating rules for this repository

These rules are **mandatory** and **non-negotiable**. They apply to GitHub
Copilot, Copilot Chat, Copilot coding agent, and to every internal LLM agent
under `src/agents/` (qa.agent.js, testAuthoring.agent.js, planner.agent.js,
executionReflection.agent.js, etc.). When a request conflicts with these
rules, refuse and propose a compliant alternative.

---

## RULE 1 — Feature files are domain-agnostic and story-driven

`tests/features/` is the **only** location for `.feature` files. Its layout
is **module-driven** where modules are derived dynamically from Jira / Zephyr
metadata via a confidence waterfall — no fixed taxonomy is hardcoded.

### Module classification (confidence waterfall)

Modules are resolved by `classifyModule()` in [src/tools/featureFile.writer.js](src/tools/featureFile.writer.js)
in this priority order:

1. Explicit caller override (`opts.module`)
2. Jira Components (`components[0].name`)
3. Jira Labels (`labels[0]`)
4. Zephyr folder name (test-management placement)
5. Story title slug (always available — last resort)

### Hard requirements

1. Every feature file MUST live at `tests/features/<module-slug>/<story-slug>.feature`.
   - `<module-slug>` — derived from Jira/Zephyr metadata via the waterfall above.
   - `<story-slug>` — derived from the story title (e.g. `employee-login-and-pim-add-employee.feature`).
2. **No** other folder shape is permitted under `tests/features/`. Specifically
   forbidden: `_unsorted/`, `misc/`, story-keyed folders, person-keyed folders,
   date-keyed folders, dump folders for "uncategorised" scenarios.
3. A scenario whose module cannot be determined falls back to `general/`.
   AI agents MUST first attempt all Jira/Zephyr metadata signals before accepting `general`.
4. Every scenario MUST carry the `@<storyKey>` tag (e.g. `@OHRM-1`), the
   `@<module-slug>` tag, and the Zephyr test case tag (`@<PROJECT>-T<id>`), plus
   design-technique tags.

### When you (the AI) generate or move feature content

- Always call `syncStoryFeature()` from [src/tools/featureFile.writer.js](src/tools/featureFile.writer.js)
  rather than writing files yourself.
- Never hardcode application-domain module names (`login`, `pim`, `leave`, etc.).
  Module names are Jira/Zephyr-metadata-derived at runtime.

---

## RULE 2 — Page Object Model MUST be strictly followed

All UI automation goes through the Page Object Model. POMs are story-specific
and generated by [scripts/generate-playwright.js](scripts/generate-playwright.js).

### Hard requirements

1. Specs under `tests/specs/`, `tests/healed/`, `tests/generated/` MUST:
   - Import `{ test, expect }` from `../fixtures/base.fixture`.
   - Use `page`, `sh`, `uniqueSuffix` from fixture arguments.
   - Use story-specific page objects from `tests/pages/<StoryClass>Page.js`
     (instantiate in the spec, not as a base fixture).
2. Specs MUST NOT contain raw selector calls. The following are
   **forbidden** in spec files:
   - `page.locator(` with a CSS/XPath/text string literal
   - `page.getByRole(`, `page.getByText(`, `page.getByLabel(`,
     `page.getByPlaceholder(`, `page.getByTestId(` called directly on `page`
   - `page.click(`, `page.fill(`, `page.type(` with a selector string
3. Story-specific page objects live in `tests/pages/<StoryClass>Page.js`.
   Locators live in `tests/locators/<StoryClass>.locators.js`.
4. Test data lives in [tests/data/](tests/data/) (`testData.js`). Never
   inline credentials, URLs, or field values into specs.
5. [scripts/generate-playwright.js](scripts/generate-playwright.js) is the
   only sanctioned way to scaffold new specs from Zephyr test cases.

### When you (the AI) author or modify a spec

Before writing, check the template in [scripts/generate-playwright.js](scripts/generate-playwright.js).
If a step needs a locator not on the relevant Page Object, **add it to the
locator class first**, then call it from the page object, then from the spec.
